name: Patient Measurement Clustering
shortDescription: Cluster patients by clinical measurement scores (KMeans, Agglomerative, Spectral)
description: Takes an OMOP-format measurement table (Parquet) and clusters patients based on their clinical scores (e.g. HAM-D, PSQI, CDR). Pivots the data so each patient becomes one row, runs KMeans clustering, and outputs the original table with a cluster label column added. Useful for identifying patient subgroups for further analysis in Superset.
inputDescription: OMOP measurement table as Parquet with columns: person_id, measurement_source_value, value_as_number

name.label: Name
name.description: Unique identifier for this node in the pipeline

algorithm.label: Clustering Algorithm
algorithm.description: Algorithm used to group patients. KMeans is fastest; Agglomerative (Ward) produces compact well-separated clusters; Spectral works well on non-convex shapes but is slower on large datasets.

n_clusters.label: Number of Clusters
n_clusters.description: How many patient groups to create. 3 clusters map to Low / Moderate / High Risk labels.

outputDescription: Original measurement table with an added 'cluster' column (integer, 0-indexed) per patient
